subject: Project Management
title: The Daily Scrum
description: Learn The Daily Scrum in one minute.

hook.text: Every day, the team meets for 15 minutes. Same time, same place. No chairs.

buildup.text: Each person answers: what did I do? What will I do? What's in my way?

discovery.text: The Daily Scrum isn't a status report — it's the team synchronizing with itself.

twist.text: If a manager sits in and asks questions, the dynamic shifts from team sync to performance review.

climax.text: Fifteen minutes of transparency replaces hours of email chains and meeting requests.

punchline.text: The shortest meeting solves the most problems.

quiz.question: What is the primary purpose of the Daily Scrum?
